name: Seizure
summary: Electrical disturbance in the brain (e.g. epilepsy). From minor twitching to full convulsions and collapse.
recognition: May have warning signs they recognise themselves

callEmergency.text: Call 999 if the seizure lasts more than a few minutes, repeats, or you don't know they're epileptic.

steps: Find something soft to place under their head if they've collapsed.
Remove hazards around them so they can't injure themselves.
Loosen tight clothing if you can.
Remove glasses if they wear them.
Wait it out — most seizures end in seconds or minutes.
If unconscious but breathing — recovery position.
Reassure when they come round — they may be confused.

warnings.text: Don't restrain them, don't put anything in their mouth, don't move them unless they're in danger.
